claim: No finite-support periodic Langton highway of transverse width four has normalized diagonal drift (m,-m) for 1 <= m <= 9.

dependencies: the four-state width-four macro transition table
flow conservation at LN and US

leaf_checks: growth equals the number of odd translation classes
every stabilized translation-class word starts R and alternates

coverage_notes: For m <= 6 the fixed-drift engine was run over the entire interval 1 <= P <= 8m; wrong-parity rows were rejected exactly.
For m = 7, the existing all-highway exclusion covers P <= 24, and fixed-drift runs cover P = 25 and every odd P from 27 through 55.
For m = 8, the existing all-highway exclusion covers P <= 24, and fixed-drift runs cover P = 26 and every even P from 28 through 64.

For m = 9, the existing all-highway exclusion covers P <= 24, the unrestricted width-four search covers P = 25 through 38, and fixed-drift runs cover every odd P from 39 through the theorem limit P = 63.
The final m = 9, P = 63 row visited 17,532,472,230 search nodes and had zero structural leaves.
The primitive m = 1 case also has a hand classification: P = 7 and six state-compatible words, each with an explicit P3 failure.

lean.scope: arithmetic kernels plus the block-key and low-crossing endpoints only; the geometric reduction and pumping splice remain on paper

nonclaim: This does not exclude width four for unbounded m and does not prove the universal highway conjecture.
